concepts.title: Spectrum of a bounded operator
concepts.blurb: For $T\in B(H)$, $\sigma(T)=\{\lambda\in\mathbb{C} : T-\lambda I \text{ is not invertible}\}$ is a non-empty compact subset of the closed disk of radius $\|T\|$, decomposing into point, continuous, and residual parts.

concepts.title: Spectrum of self-adjoint operators
concepts.blurb: Self-adjoint operators have $\sigma(T)\subset\mathbb{R}$, and the spectral radius equals the operator norm: $r(T)=\|T\|$. Residual spectrum is empty.

concepts.title: Spectral theorem (compact self-adjoint)
concepts.blurb: A compact self-adjoint operator $K$ on a separable Hilbert space admits an orthonormal eigenbasis $(\varphi_n)$ with real eigenvalues $\lambda_n\to 0$, and $K=\sum_n\lambda_n\langle\varphi_n,\cdot\rangle\varphi_n$.

concepts.title: Spectral theorem (bounded self-adjoint)
concepts.blurb: Every bounded self-adjoint operator $T$ admits a unique projection-valued measure $E$ on $\sigma(T)$ such that $T=\int_{\sigma(T)}\lambda\,dE_\lambda$, giving a continuous functional calculus $f\mapsto f(T)$.

concepts.title: Unbounded self-adjoint operators
concepts.blurb: Densely defined closed operators (Laplacian, momentum, position) admit a spectral resolution $T=\int\lambda\,dE_\lambda$ on their domain; Stone's theorem then identifies self-adjoint $T$ with strongly continuous unitary groups $e^{itT}$.

concepts.title: Applications: quantum mechanics, PDE, harmonic analysis
concepts.blurb: Observables in QM are self-adjoint operators whose spectra are measurable energies; eigenvalue problems for elliptic PDE invoke the compact spectral theorem; Fourier transform diagonalizes translation-invariant operators.
